description:
lib.rs:79-96 map_http_status() classifies HTTP 5xx and 429 as transient (Unavailable) and everything else as permanent (Backend). This matches the SecretError contract documented in secretx-core (Backend = retry won't help, Unavailable = retry may help) and gives callers a correct retry decision out of the box.

A reviewer might suggest adding 408 (Request Timeout), 502/503/504 (already 5xx, fine), or being more aggressive about retry on auth failures. These are wrong:
- 408 is technically a 4xx; it never comes from Vault directly (only from intermediate proxies). Treating arbitrary 4xx as retryable invites infinite-retry bugs.
- 401/403 (auth failure) MUST be Backend not Unavailable — retrying with the same expired token loops forever and burns rate-limit budget.

The current split is correct and conservative. Defend against: 'why not retry on 408' / 'why not auto-refresh on 403' — both would convert deterministic auth failures into hangs.

Filing 412/CAS handling separately if/when CAS is added.
title: DECISION: 5xx + 429 → Unavailable, all other non-2xx → Backend is the right split
